{{Quote|By the time we're through with her, the ''Jade Shadow'' will put the ''Falcon'' to shame.|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Jade Shadow''''' was a heavily modified [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[Horizon-class star yacht|''Horizon''-class Star Yacht]] owned by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], which replaced her previous ship, ''[[Jade Sabre]]''.

==Characteristics==
Like thousands of star yachts that cruised [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the ''Jade Shadow'' was built around a sturdy frame that was fairly wide at the engines and tapered off until it reached the narrow, triangular cockpit. Although the ship was equipped with several support features to allow Mara to operate the starship alone, the bridge was designed for a pilot, copilot, and navigator. Above the cockpit was a large, [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] canopy that provided breathtaking views of space, and a crystal clear image of hostile starships.

A pair of retractable [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] AG-1G [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] were installed with help from [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Talon Karrde]]. The cannons were mounted on turrets which allowed them to have a wide firing arc, and were equipped with stutter-fire capabilities. They also equipped the vessel with two concealed [[Dymek/Legends|Dymek]] HM-8 [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] launchers, each carrying magazines of eight high-yield missiles. The ship also sported a retractable blaster cannon.

Many of the ''Jade Shadow''{{'s}} systems were voice-activated. When piloting the craft, system data was displayed on unobtrusive holograms synced to a retinal tracker, which positioned these readouts just outside the pilot's forward line of sight. All of the weapons systems on board were connected to a central targeting computer that was configured with many different options and settings; one of the most notable was the "shoot-back" setting, which directed the ship's targeting computer to automatically shoot down any craft that opened fire on the ship. Mara and her husband, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], also added an [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]] system, which was programmed with a variety of evasive maneuvers. Captain Han Solo added an advanced long-range sensor suite, complete with port and starboard visual scanners, jamming devices, sensor decoys and false transponder codes, which allowed the ship to slip through defenses without revealing who her true owner was. Mara also added a powerful, albeit costly, [[HoloNet/Legends|holographic communications]] array that had enough range to send and receive messages from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Like Mara's previous ships, the ship was equipped with a remote-controlled [[slave circuit]], that could summon it over short distances.

Han Solo worked to increase the ''Jade Shadow''{{'s}} speed, fine-tuning the twin ion drives and reworking the hyperdrive to allow speed on par with that of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. However, the Jade Shadow's towing capacity was more limited due to her yacht-class drive units.

Luke and Mara Skywalker reconfigured the aft docking bay to contain her modified [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunter]] starfighter or even carry an entire [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], the preferred starfighter of her husband. The ''Jade Shadow'' also allowed for an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] to plug into and fly the ship at a droid station located on the flight deck.

==History==
After her previous ship, ''[[Jade Sabre]]'', was destroyed on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invasion of that planet, Mara Jade Skywalker found herself without a ship—but that situation would not last long. Within a few months of the attack on Dantooine, Lando Calrissian gave Mara the gift of a [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] space yacht that he had recently purchased from [[Spice/Legends|spice]] merchants. Calrissian's wife, [[Tendra Risant Calrissian|Tendra]], dubbed the ship ''Jade Shadow'' because of its non-reflective gray hull. While most star yachts were used for pleasure, in the chaos of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Mara and her allies joined forces to transform an ordinary, utilitarian vessel into a powerful tool for the [[New Jedi Order]]. From thereon, the Jade Shadow would remain in Mara's possession as her personal starship.

During the [[Dark Nest Crisis]], Mara, Luke, and their son, [[Ben Skywalker|Ben]], traveled in the ''Jade Shadow'' to [[Qoribu]] in an attempt to negotiate peace between the [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] colonies and the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]]. During the trip, Ben overheard Luke using vulgar language as he fiddled with a malfunctioning [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], to the embarrassment of the elder Skywalker.

In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Mara and Luke took the ''Jade Shadow'' to [[Roqoo Depot]] to [[Duel on Roqoo Depot|confront]] Sith Lady [[Lumiya]] and renegade [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alema Rar]]. Mara was murdered by her corrupted nephew, [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], later that year, and the ''Jade Shadow'' remained berthed for the remainder of the war. [[43 ABY|Two years later]], the ship became the mobile home of Luke and Ben after the former was exiled by [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Natasi Daala]], and saw action against the resurgent [[Lost Tribe of Sith]].
